notes_on_cap: Pre-FA figure (~$11M) per ESPN accounting for $48M in potential dead money from voiding contracts of Jordan/Davis/Hill/Moreau. Post-FA OTC figure reflects new league year opening. Dead cap absorbed from Jordan ($18.76M), Davis ($14.29M), Hill ($13.72M), Moreau ($1.4M) voids partially offset by freed salary.

key_departures.impact: Jordan hit free agency for the first time in his 15-year career after the Saints could not agree on a new deal before the new league year. He had 10.5 sacks in 2025 — his most since 2021 — and was the unquestioned starter and defensive leader. The Saints absorb $18.76M in dead money. No EDGE replacement signed as of the update date. Jordan plans to play a 16th season elsewhere.

Davis spent eight seasons as the defensive leader in New Orleans, earning multiple Pro Bowl honors. He signed with the Jets for 2yrs/$22M with $15M guaranteed — a meaningful departure that creates a significant hole at off-ball LB. The Saints directly responded by signing Kaden Elliss.

Taylor was the Saints' top cornerback — particularly valuable in the slot — and commanded top CB1 money ($20M/yr AAV) on the open market. His loss to Tennessee on a 3yr/$60M deal escalates the Saints' CB need significantly. The compensatory pick benefit is a silver lining but the hole is real.

key_departures.trade_context: Shaheed was traded by the Saints to Seattle at the November 2025 trade deadline for 2026 4th- and 5th-round picks. He then re-signed with Seattle in free agency.
key_departures.impact: The Saints traded Shaheed mid-season and collected 4th/5th round picks. He was a dual-threat WR/returner with 138 career catches for 2,055 yards in New Orleans. His loss predates FA but is captured here as a net departure. The Saints lose a dynamic playmaker and elite returner.

Fortner served as a backup center behind Erik McCoy. His departure to Carolina is a depth loss that slightly thins the interior OL room, though McCoy remains the starter and the Saints added Edwards at LG.

key_departures.trade_details: Traded to Houston along with a 2028 7th-round pick in exchange for a 2028 6th-round pick
key_departures.impact: Kroeger averaged only 37.3 net yards per punt, ranking near the bottom of the league. The Saints actively moved on from him and immediately replaced with Ryan Wright.

Hill's contract voided at the start of the new league year. He is widely expected to retire following a knee injury that diminished his role late in his Saints career. His departure, combined with Foster Moreau's, thinned the TE room — addressed partially by Noah Fant signing.
Moreau was a rotational blocking tight end. His contract voided. The Saints added Fant to help compensate, but the TE depth room is thinner.

contract.notes: Guarantee amount not yet confirmed in reporting; Spotrac lists $13M AAV. NFL.com confirms 4yr/$52M. Guarantees TBD pending full contract filing.

signings.analysis: Etienne is a proven 1,000-yard rusher who also contributes in the passing game (36 catches, 292 yards, 6 TDs receiving in 2025). The Saints' rushing attack ranked 28th in YPG and 31st in yards per carry in 2025 — this is a direct, high-investment fix. At $13M/yr on a 4-year deal, it's top-of-market for the position tier. The 4-year commitment signals a genuine starter investment, not a bridge. Alvin Kamara (30, coming off injury) now faces a likely backup/trade scenario. RB need is resolved for the foreseeable future.

Edwards was the top guard on the 2026 free agent market. The $45M fully guaranteed and $20M signing bonus on a 4-year deal is the Saints' clearest commitment of the offseason. LG was a revolving door for years — now it's locked up with a Super Bowl-winning starter who hasn't missed a game due to injury in Buffalo. At $15.25M AAV, this is top-of-market for IOL. Left guard need is fully addressed with a durable, proven starter in his prime (turns 29 at signing). OL need drops significantly — center (McCoy) remains, but the IOL picture is meaningfully improved.

Fant is a short-term depth addition to partner with Juwan Johnson (the clear TE1 who had a 77-catch, 889-yard season in 2025). At $4.375M AAV and 2 years, this is below-starter money — a rotational piece and 12-personnel option. He brings run-blocking ability (82.3% win rate on 142 blocking plays in 2025) and red-zone upside, but the fumble issues (3 fumbles returned for TDs) are a concern. With Taysom Hill and Foster Moreau's contracts voiding, this partially addresses the TE depth need but does NOT solve the TE2 need at a starter level. TE need remains moderate.

Elliss is a returning Saint originally drafted by the team in 2019. He became a full-time starter in Atlanta, recording 107 tackles and 3.5 sacks in 2025, and hasn't missed a game in four seasons. The 3yr/$33M deal with $23M guaranteed is a legitimate starter-level investment at LB. However, replacing Demario Davis (a multi-time Pro Bowler and defensive captain) with Elliss is a step down in star power and leadership — though Elliss is a capable starter in his own right. LB need is partially addressed: Elliss is a solid starter, but Davis was more impactful and the transition involves a leadership void. Pete Werner and Danny Stutsman remain as depth behind Elliss.

The Saints had one of the worst punting situations in the NFL in 2025 (Kroeger: 37.3 net yards/punt, bottom of the league) and had cycled through multiple punters since parting ways with Thomas Morstead. Wright gets a 4-year commitment with $8M guaranteed — significant investment for a punter, reflecting how seriously the Saints take addressing this chronic weakness. Punting need is fully resolved.

Ridgeway is a depth IDL signing — the Saints gave up a Day 3 pick to acquire him (per ESPN). He provides run-defense depth but missed significant time on IR in 2025. At $3.1M AAV with only $1.65M guaranteed, this is a camp-competition/rotational signing. IDL remains a genuine need at the starter level.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Cameron Jordan (10.5 sacks, all-time franchise sack leader) departed as a free agent with no replacement signed. The Saints have zero proven starters at EDGE entering the draft. Chase Young is the only depth option. This is now the most critical need on the roster and should be a top priority at pick No. 8 or via trade if an elite prospect is available.

Alontae Taylor signed a 3yr/$60M deal with Tennessee — top CB1 money. He was the Saints' best corner and their primary slot defender. No CB was added in free agency. This has become a co-equal need with EDGE heading into the draft. The Saints need at least one starter-caliber CB, ideally in rounds 1-2.

Travis Etienne signing (4yr/$52M) fully addresses the RB need. He is a proven 1,000-yard rusher with receiving chops who fills the exact production gap the Saints had. Alvin Kamara remains on the roster (possible trade candidate) with Kendre Miller and Devin Neal as depth. RB is no longer a draft priority.

David Edwards signing (4yr/$61M, $45M guaranteed) as the top guard on the market fully addresses the LG need that had been a multi-year problem. Erik McCoy remains the starting center. Interior OL is now a depth-only consideration — no longer a premium draft need. A developmental IOL on Day 3 is reasonable but not urgent.

Demario Davis departed (Jets, 2yr/$22M), creating a starter-level void. Kaden Elliss (3yr/$33M, $23M guaranteed) partially fills it — he's a capable full-time starter who doesn't miss games. But Davis was a Pro Bowl leader and the downgrade in defensive command is real. LB moves from need to moderate need. Pete Werner and Danny Stutsman provide depth. Still a target in rounds 3-5.

Rashid Shaheed was traded away in November 2025 and signed with Seattle. No WR was added in free agency. Chris Olave is the WR1 but no other wideout on the roster is clearly a starter-caliber threat. The Saints need at least one WR2, ideally in rounds 1-3. With EDGE and CB competing for top-of-board attention, WR could be a value pick on Day 2.

John Ridgeway provides minimal depth but is not a starter. The Saints still lack a push-the-pocket IDL starter opposite David Onyemata. No meaningful upgrade was made at this position. IDL remains a genuine need in rounds 2-4.

Juwan Johnson (77 catches, 889 yards in 2025) is the TE1 on a recent extension. Noah Fant provides depth/rotational help on a 2yr/$8.75M deal. But Hill and Moreau are gone. Fant's two-year, low-guarantee deal signals a bridge, not a solution. TE2 remains a moderate need, particularly for a true pass-catching option. Rounds 3-5 target.

No OT signing was made in early free agency. With a young QB in Tyler Shough behind a line that still has questions at tackle, OT remains a legitimate need. The Edwards signing helps run blocking by improving the interior, but the tackle spots are unchanged. Still a rounds 2-3 target.

key_additions.headline: Louisiana native returns home; 1,000-yard rusher fixes a bottom-5 running game
Top guard on the market ends Saints' years-long revolving door at left guard
Original Saints 7th-round pick returns to replace Demario Davis after 3 seasons as Falcons starter
Former 1st-round pick adds 12-personnel depth and blocking upside alongside Juwan Johnson
Four-year investment ends chronic punter instability; Kroeger-era bottom-5 net average replaced
Depth DT adds to interior rotation; run defense insurance after IR-shortened 2025 season

key_losses.headline: Saints franchise sack leader (132 career) departs as free agent for first time in 15-year career; $18.76M dead cap absorbed
8-season Saints defensive captain and Pro Bowler returns to Jets; biggest leadership void of the offseason
Top cornerback cashes in at $20M/yr in Tennessee; Saints secondary loses its best defender

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded Nov. 2025 (4th/5th picks received); re-signed SEA 3yr/$51M
key_losses.headline: Dynamic speedster and elite returner traded mid-season and re-signed by Super Bowl champion Seahawks
Beloved gadget player's contract voided; knee injury led to diminished role and expected retirement
Backup center departs to Carolina; depth loss behind Erik McCoy

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to HOU for pick swap (2028 7th for 2028 6th)
key_losses.headline: Struggled rookie punter (37.3 net YPP) traded away as Saints upgrade with Ryan Wright

confidence_notes: All signings confirmed by multiple credible outlets (ESPN, NFL Network, Spotrac, OverTheCap). Contract guarantees for Travis Etienne not yet fully disclosed — total value (4yr/$52M, $13M AAV) confirmed but guarantee figure listed as TBD. All other contract structures sourced from Spotrac/OTC/beat reporters. Cameron Jordan's new team not yet confirmed as of 2026-03-11 — he is an unsigned free agent.
